summary.brief: Hodes & Nauser, MDs v. Kobach. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The district court found the only evidence offered demonstrated there was 'no reasonable alternative' to that procedure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the district court's order.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The district court granted the temporary injunction after concluding the Providers were substantially likely to prevail on their claim that S.B. 95 violates the Kansas Constitution Bill of Rights.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Providers disclosed three fact and expert witnesses: Plaintiff Dr. Nauser; Dr. Anne Davis, a medical expert in obstetrics and gynecology; and Dr. Thomas Cunningham, an expert in clinical ethics, bioethics, philosophical ethics, and philosophy of medicine.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On April 7, 2021, the district court granted summary judgment for the Providers.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It found that doctors in Kansas perform the D & E between 14 weeks from the last menstrual period and viability, and that it is the safest and most common form of second-trimester abortion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court found generally that these alternatives are more dangerous, untested, or would be impossible in some cases.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The State failed to prove it has a compelling interest in 'regulating the medical profession and maintaining the ethical integrity of the medical profession.'
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: S.B. 95 is not narrowly tailored to the State's compelling interest in promoting respect for the value and dignity of human life, born and unborn.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is appropriate when the pleadings, depositions, answers to interrogatories, admissions on file, and supporting affidavits show that no genuine issue exists as to any material fact and the moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: S.B. 95 does not 'provide explicit standards' for enforcement and, therefore, is unconstitutionally vague.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Expert Witness Testimony
requestables.description: Testimony from Dr. Anne Davis, a medical expert in obstetrics and gynecology

reveal.result_summary: D & E is the safest and most common form of second-trimester abortion
reveal.clinical_significance: Supports the safety and commonality of the D & E procedure
reveal.decision_impact: Should inform the decision on the appropriateness of D & E

timeline_phases.name: Legislative Action
timeline_phases.description: Enactment of S.B. 95 banning D & E procedure

decision_points.prompt: What should be the legal approach to the D & E procedure?

options.description: Allow D & E procedure as it is the safest and most common method
options.clinical_reasoning: Based on expert testimony and safety data

options.description: Ban D & E procedure and propose alternatives
options.clinical_reasoning: Alternatives are more dangerous and untested

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant supported the ban on D & E
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Belief in promoting respect for the value and dignity of human life

expected_action_court.description: D & E should be allowed based on safety and commonality

explanation.what_should_have_happened: D & E should have been allowed as per expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient received D & E abortion without complications

key_findings: D & E is a safe and common procedure
No breach of duty in offering D & E

court_findings.finding: State failed to prove compelling interest
D & E is a safe and common procedure
